claim_boundary: Tier 7.1f is a contract/family-selection gate only. It does not download NAB, score CRA, compare models, freeze a baseline, authorize hardware/native transfer, or prove public usefulness.
next_step: Tier 7.1g NAB source/data/scoring preflight: verify source access, source hash/commit, file/label parse, label-separated streams, tiny leakage-safe smoke rows, and scoring-interface feasibility before any full NAB scoring.

metrics: NAB Standard profile score if scorer integration is practical
NAB reward-low-FP and reward-low-FN scores if scorer integration is practical
pointwise AUROC / AUPRC as secondary metrics

official_sources.license_or_source_note: MIT license shown by GitHub repository metadata; repository includes data, labels, scoring tools, and README.
official_sources.name: Numenta NAB GitHub repository

official_sources.license_or_source_note: Use files from the official repository only; preserve source commit/hash in preflight.
official_sources.name: NAB data directory

official_sources.license_or_source_note: Labels are scoring-only; no online detector may read labels before emitting anomaly scores.
official_sources.name: NAB labels directory

official_sources.license_or_source_note: Use for benchmark/scoring description and citation.
official_sources.name: Evaluating Real-time Anomaly Detection Algorithms - the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark

fail_or_park: CRA only wins one cherry-picked file/category
preflight_pass: official source is reachable or cached with documented checksum
scoring labels are separated from online streams
no label leakage in detector rows
scoring_candidate_pass: CRA beats or complements the strongest reproduced baseline on predeclared primary metric
confidence intervals or bootstrap intervals support the result

preprocessing_contract: download or clone official NAB source only
record source URL, commit or release tag if available, checksum/hash manifest
normalize using train/calibration prefix only when a model requires normalization
keep anomaly labels in a separate scoring file
no online model may access future labels, future points, or anomaly windows before scoring
predeclare file subsets before scoring; tiny subsets are smoke only
selected_adapter.rationale: C-MAPSS behaved like a monotone remaining-useful-life regression problem, which is not the strongest pressure for CRA's memory/adaptation path. NAB is a public streaming anomaly benchmark with labeled anomaly windows, real-time scoring, false-positive pressure, and nonstationary time-series streams. It better targets online prediction error, surprise, adaptation, and recovery without moving hardware prematurely.
required_baselines: CRA v2.2/v2.3 prediction-error or state-surprise detector
CRA shams: shuffled state, no-update, no-memory, wrong-threshold calibration
published NAB scoreboard values as citation/reference only, not direct rerun unless reproduced

tier: Tier 7.1f - Next Public Adapter Contract / Family Selection
